name: Lebkuchen or German Honey Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Powdered sugar plus 2 tablespoons Water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine the honey and sugar. Beat until fully blended.
2. Add the egg and beat very well. Stir in the lemon juice.
3. Gradually add the flour mixture (baking soda, nutmeg, cinnamon, cloves, and allspice) to the honey mixture. Mix thoroughly.
4. Gently stir in the candied citrus. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 6 hours. The dough will be easier to roll out when well chilled.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease cookie sheets.
6. On a lightly floured surface, roll out half of the dough into a large rectangle about 1/4 inch thick, sprinkling flour as needed to prevent sticking.
7. Cut the dough into rectangles approximately 1-1/2 x 2-1/2 inches. Place them on prepared cookie sheets about 1 inch apart.
8. Bake for about 10 to 12 minutes, or until the edges are slightly colored.
9. Remove from the oven and gently lift the cookies onto wire racks to cool.
10. While the cookies are still warm, brush with a Clear Glaze if desired. Store airtight for several days so the cookies can mellow.
11. For the clear glaze: Combine powdered sugar and water until smooth. Brush or spoon the glaze onto warm cookies.
